frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: encoding accuracy optimized toward conditional orientation statistics within the specific surround context

frameworks.name: Fisher information
frameworks.one_line: lawful bias-variance-Fisher link used to recover encoding accuracy from behavior

frameworks.name: contextual gain control
frameworks.one_line: tilt-illusion-style surround modulation of sensory neuron gain and tuning

core_question: Can contextual modulations of sensory encoding be characterized directly from psychophysical behavior in a tilt-illusion task, with minimal decoding assumptions?
key_assumptions: perceptual bias, variance, and Fisher information are lawfully related
decoding requires only minimal assumptions when this lawful relation is exploited
encoding accuracy reflects the conditional stimulus statistics of the current context
manipulations: surround type: non-oriented filtered noise vs oriented grating
surround orientation: +30 vs -30 degrees off vertical
probe orientation estimated via line-rotation report
key_findings: with non-oriented surround, encoding accuracy tracked natural-scene orientation statistics
with oriented surround, encoding accuracy was boosted at the surround orientation
encoding accuracy was slightly reduced for orientations neighboring the surround
interpretations: contextual modulation of sensory encoding implements a form of efficient coding tuned to conditional context statistics
the bias-variance-Fisher method generalizes to estimating encoding accuracy for other perceptual variables and contexts
